mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate (below 1%) keeps the equity risk premium compressed via low discount rates, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.38 as of 2016-02-01, near zero-bound levels
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during periods with FEDFUNDS<1% is not statistically greater than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC<0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions, defined as UNRATE rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, signals an emerging growth slowdown that depresses forward equity returns over the subsequent month as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.9 as of 2016-02-01, stable near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average triggered-return is not significantly below baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth and industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals broad-based real economic momentum that is not yet priced into equities, producing above-average S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; neither series alone is sufficient because payroll strength without production growth reflects services-only expansion with weaker earnings pass-through.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=143560 thousand, INDPRO=106.85 as of Jan 2016 vintage
failure_condition: Interaction term (PAYEMS 3m growth x INDPRO 3m growth) shows Rank IC below single-factor PAYEMS-only model over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no incremental interaction value.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where the federal funds rate has begun rising from cycle lows, a decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals the interest-rate-sensitive demand sector is weakening first, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as the downturn broadens to other sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over trailing 3 months while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1092 thousand units (revised) as of Jan 2016
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle beginning late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 60-day forward return, or the effect disappears once conditioned only on HOUST without FEDFUNDS rising (i.e., interaction adds no explanatory power).

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing sector output and capacity utilization, which is a leading indicator of near-term corporate earnings strength, producing modest positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage=106.85 vs revised=99.56, showing large revision risk as of Jan 2016
failure_condition: 5-day forward return Rank IC conditioned on INDPRO 3-month trend is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 0.5% signals weakening consumer demand, which given consumption's dominant share of GDP, foreshadows downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 20-day return not significantly different from baseline (p>0.10), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below the inflation rate, real interest rates turn increasingly negative, incentivizing capital flow into equities as a real-asset hedge, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this mechanism requires the joint condition since rising inflation alone without a negative real rate gap does not reliably compress discount rates.
regime_hint: negative real rate environment / financial repression
failure_condition: Median 60-day forward return during triggered negative-real-rate periods is not significantly higher than unconditional median (p>0.05), or effect reverses sign in more than one 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, an unemployment rate (UNRATE) falling below prior 12-month lows signals labor market overheating that raises market expectations of faster Fed rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as discount rate expectations rise abruptly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE hits new 12-month low while FEDFUNDS trend is rising
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.9 near multi-year lows as of Feb 2016, FEDFUNDS just hiked from near-zero in Dec 2015
regime_hint: early monetary tightening cycle following zero-rate period
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following new UNRATE lows during tightening cycles are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.
